title: rubus serpens var. chlorostachys
intro: Rubus serpens var. chlorostachys, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or creeping raspberry, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is native to the Himalayan region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Rubus serpens var. chlorostachys include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
